NKJV.1: By the rivers of Babylon, There we sat down, yea, we wept When we remembered Zion.
NKJV.2: We hung our harps Upon the willows in the midst of it.
NKJV.3: For there those who carried us away captive asked of us a song, And those who plundered us requested mirth, Saying, “Sing us one of the songs of Zion!”
NKJV.4: How shall we sing the LORD’s song In a foreign land?
NKJV.5: If I forget you, O Jerusalem, Let my right hand forget its skill!
NKJV.6: If I do not remember you, Let my tongue cling to the roof of my mouth— If I do not exalt Jerusalem Above my chief joy.
NKJV.7: Remember, O LORD, against the sons of Edom The day of Jerusalem, Who said, “Raze it, raze it, To its very foundation!”
NKJV.8: O daughter of Babylon, who are to be destroyed, Happy the one who repays you as you have served us!
NKJV.9: Happy the one who takes and dashes Your little ones against the rock!

KJV.1: By the rivers<S>5104</S> of Babylon<S>894</S>, there we sat down<S>3427</S>, yea, we wept<S>1058</S>, when we remembered<S>2142</S> Zion<S>6726</S>.
KJV.2: We hanged<S>8518</S> our harps<S>3658</S> upon the willows<S>6155</S> in the midst<S>8432</S> thereof.
KJV.3: For there they that carried us away captive<S>7617</S> required<S>7592</S> of us a song<S>1697</S> <S>7892</S>; and they that wasted<S>8437</S> us required of us mirth<S>8057</S>, saying, Sing<S>7891</S> us one of the songs<S>7892</S> of Zion<S>6726</S>. <sup>a song: Heb. the words of a song</sup> <sup>wasted: Heb. laid us on heaps</sup>
KJV.4: How shall we sing<S>7891</S> the LORD'S<S>3068</S> song<S>7892</S> in a strange<S>5236</S> land<S>127</S>? <sup>strange: Heb. land of a stranger?</sup>
KJV.5: If I forget<S>7911</S> thee, O Jerusalem<S>3389</S>, let my right hand<S>3225</S> forget<S>7911</S> her cunning.
KJV.6: If I do not remember<S>2142</S> thee, let my tongue<S>3956</S> cleave<S>1692</S> to the roof of my mouth<S>2441</S>; if I prefer<S>5927</S> not Jerusalem<S>3389</S> above my chief<S>7218</S> joy<S>8057</S>. <sup>my chief: Heb. the head of my joy</sup>
KJV.7: Remember<S>2142</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, the children<S>1121</S> of Edom<S>123</S> in the day<S>3117</S> of Jerusalem<S>3389</S>; who said<S>559</S>, Rase<S>6168</S> it, rase<S>6168</S> it, even to the foundation<S>3247</S> thereof. <sup>Rase it: Heb. Make bare</sup>
KJV.8: O daughter<S>1323</S> of Babylon<S>894</S>, who art to be destroyed<S>7703</S>; happy<S>835</S> shall he be, that rewardeth<S>7999</S> thee as thou hast served<S>1580</S> <S>1576</S> us. <sup>destroyed: Heb. wasted</sup> <sup>rewardeth: Heb. recompenseth unto thee thy deed which thou didst to us</sup>
KJV.9: Happy<S>835</S> shall he be, that taketh<S>270</S> and dasheth<S>5310</S> thy little ones<S>5768</S> against the stones<S>5553</S>. <sup>the stones: Heb. the rock</sup>

ESV.1: By the waters of Babylon, there we sat down and wept, when we remembered Zion.
ESV.2: On the willows  there we hung up our lyres.
ESV.3: For there our captors required of us songs, and our tormentors, mirth, saying, “Sing us one of the songs of Zion!”
ESV.4: How shall we sing the LORD's song in a foreign land?
ESV.5: If I forget you, O Jerusalem, let my right hand forget its skill!
ESV.6: Let my tongue stick to the roof of my mouth, if I do not remember you, if I do not set Jerusalem above my highest joy!
ESV.7: Remember, O LORD, against the Edomites the day of Jerusalem, how they said, “Lay it bare, lay it bare, down to its foundations!”
ESV.8: O daughter of Babylon, doomed to be destroyed, blessed shall he be who repays you with what you have done to us!
ESV.9: Blessed shall he be who takes your little ones and dashes them against the rock!

NASB.1: By the rivers of Babylon, There we sat down and wept, When we remembered Zion.
NASB.2: Upon the willows in the midst of it We hung our harps.
NASB.3: For there our captors demanded of us songs, And our tormentors mirth, [saying], 'Sing us one of the songs of Zion.'
NASB.4: How can we sing the LORD'S song In a foreign land?
NASB.5: If I forget you, O Jerusalem, May my right hand forget [her skill].
NASB.6: May my tongue cling to the roof of my mouth If I do not remember you, If I do not exalt Jerusalem Above my chief joy.
NASB.7: Remember, O LORD, against the sons of Edom The day of Jerusalem, Who said, 'Raze it, raze it To its very foundation.'
NASB.8: O daughter of Babylon, you devastated one, How blessed will be the one who repays you With the recompense with which you have repaid us.
NASB.9: How blessed will be the one who seizes and dashes your little ones Against the rock.

NLT.1:
Beside the rivers of Babylon, we sat and wept
as we thought of Jerusalem.
NLT.2:
We put away our harps,
hanging them on the branches of poplar trees.
NLT.3:
For our captors demanded a song from us.
Our tormentors insisted on a joyful hymn:
“Sing us one of those songs of Jerusalem!”
NLT.4:
But how can we sing the songs of the LORD
while in a pagan land?
NLT.5:
If I forget you, O Jerusalem,
let my right hand forget how to play the harp.
NLT.6:
May my tongue stick to the roof of my mouth
if I fail to remember you,
if I don’t make Jerusalem my greatest joy.
NLT.7:
O LORD, remember what the Edomites did
on the day the armies of Babylon captured Jerusalem.
“Destroy it!” they yelled.
“Level it to the ground!”
NLT.8:
O Babylon, you will be destroyed.
Happy is the one who pays you back
for what you have done to us.
NLT.9:
Happy is the one who takes your babies
and smashes them against the rocks!

WEB.1: By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat down. Yes, we wept, when we remembered Zion.
WEB.2: On the willows in that land, we hung up our harps.
WEB.3: For there, those who led us captive asked us for songs. Those who tormented us demanded songs of joy: “Sing us one of the songs of Zion!”
WEB.4: How can we sing Yahweh’s song in a foreign land?
WEB.5: If I forget you, Jerusalem, let my right hand forget its skill.
WEB.6: Let my tongue stick to the roof of my mouth if I don’t remember you; if I don’t prefer Jerusalem above my chief joy.
WEB.7: Remember, Yahweh, against the children of Edom, the day of Jerusalem; who said, “Raze it! Raze it even to its foundation!”
WEB.8: Daughter of Babylon, doomed to destruction, he will be happy who rewards you, as you have served us.
WEB.9: Happy shall he be, who takes and dashes your little ones against the rock.

CSB17.1:
By the rivers of Babylon —
there we sat down and wept
when we remembered Zion. ⓘ
CSB17.2:
There we hung up our lyres
on the poplar trees, ⓙ
CSB17.3:
for our captors there asked us for songs,
and our tormentors, for rejoicing:
“Sing us one of the songs of Zion.” ⓚ
CSB17.4:
How can we sing the LORD’s song
on foreign soil? ⓛ
CSB17.5:
If I forget you, Jerusalem,
may my right hand forget its skill. ⓜ
CSB17.6:
May my tongue stick to the roof of my mouth
if I do not remember you,
if I do not exalt Jerusalem as my greatest joy! ⓝ
CSB17.7:
Remember, LORD, what the Edomites said
that day [1] at Jerusalem:
“Destroy it! Destroy it
down to its foundations!” ⓐ
CSB17.8:
Daughter Babylon, doomed to destruction,
happy is the one who pays you back
what you have done to us. ⓑ
CSB17.9:
Happy is he who takes your little ones
and dashes them against the rocks. ⓒ

AMP.1:
By the rivers of Babylon,
There we [captives] sat down and wept,
When we remembered Zion [the city God imprinted on our hearts].
AMP.2:
On the willow trees in the midst of Babylon
We hung our harps.
AMP.3:
For there they who took us captive demanded of us a song with words,
And our tormentors [who made a mockery of us demanded] amusement, saying,
"Sing us one of the songs of Zion."
AMP.4:
How can we sing the Lord's song
In a strange and foreign land?
AMP.5:
If I forget you, O Jerusalem,
Let my right hand forget [her skill with the harp].
AMP.6:
Let my tongue cling to the roof of my mouth
If I do not remember you,
If I do not prefer Jerusalem
Above my chief joy.
AMP.7:
Remember, O LORD, against the sons of Edom,
The day of [the fall of] Jerusalem,
Who said "Down, down [with her]
To her very foundation."
AMP.8:
O daughter of Babylon, you devastator,
How blessed will be the one
Who repays you [with destruction] as you have repaid us.
AMP.9:
How blessed will be the one who seizes and dashes your little ones
Against the rock.

MSG.1:
Alongside Babylon's rivers
we sat on the banks; we cried and cried,
remembering the good old days in Zion.
MSG.2:
Alongside the quaking aspens
we stacked our unplayed harps;
MSG.3:
That's where our captors demanded songs,
sarcastic and mocking:
"Sing us a happy Zion song!"
MSG.4:
Oh, how could we ever sing God's song
in this wasteland?
MSG.5:
If I ever forget you, Jerusalem,
let my fingers wither and fall off like leaves.
MSG.6:
Let my tongue swell and turn black
if I fail to remember you,
If I fail, O dear Jerusalem,
to honor you as my greatest.
MSG.7:
God, remember those Edomites,
and remember the ruin of Jerusalem,
That day they yelled out,
"Wreck it, smash it to bits!"
MSG.8:
And you, Babylonians—ravagers!
A reward to whoever gets back at you
for all you've done to us;
MSG.9:
Yes, a reward to the one who grabs your babies
and smashes their heads on the rocks!

NIV.1:
Psalm 137
By the rivers of Babylon we sat and wept
when we remembered Zion.
NIV.2:
There on the poplars
we hung our harps,
NIV.3:
for there our captors asked us for songs,
our tormentors demanded songs of joy;
they said, “Sing us one of the songs of Zion!”
NIV.4:
How can we sing the songs of the Lord
while in a foreign land?
NIV.5:
If I forget you, O Jerusalem,
may my right hand forget its skill.
NIV.6:
May my tongue cling to the roof of my mouth
if I do not remember you,
if I do not consider Jerusalem
my highest joy.
NIV.7:
Remember, O Lord, what the Edomites did
on the day Jerusalem fell.
“Tear it down,” they cried,
“tear it down to its foundations!”
NIV.8:
O Daughter of Babylon, doomed to destruction,
happy is he who repays you
for what you have done to us—
NIV.9:
he who seizes your infants
and dashes them against the rocks.

NRSVCE.1:
By the rivers of Babylon —
there we sat down and there we wept
when we remembered Zion.
NRSVCE.2:
On the willows there
we hung up our harps.
NRSVCE.3:
For there our captors
asked us for songs,
and our tormentors asked for mirth, saying,
“Sing us one of the songs of Zion!”
NRSVCE.4:
How could we sing the LORD’s song
in a foreign land?
NRSVCE.5:
If I forget you, O Jerusalem,
let my right hand wither!
NRSVCE.6:
Let my tongue cling to the roof of my mouth,
if I do not remember you,
if I do not set Jerusalem
above my highest joy.
NRSVCE.7:
Remember, O LORD, against the Edomites
the day of Jerusalem’s fall,
how they said, “Tear it down! Tear it down!
Down to its foundations!”
NRSVCE.8:
O daughter Babylon, you devastator!
Happy shall they be who pay you back
what you have done to us!
NRSVCE.9:
Happy shall they be who take your little ones
and dash them against the rock!

NET.1: By the rivers of Babylon we sit down and weep when we remember Zion.
NET.2: On the poplars in her midst we hang our harps,
NET.3: for there our captors ask us to compose songs; those who mock us demand that we be happy, saying: “Sing for us a song about Zion!”
NET.4: How can we sing a song to the LORD in a foreign land?
NET.5: If I forget you, O Jerusalem, may my right hand be crippled!
NET.6: May my tongue stick to the roof of my mouth, if I do not remember you, and do not give Jerusalem priority over whatever gives me the most joy.
NET.7: Remember, O LORD, what the Edomites did on the day Jerusalem fell. They said, “Tear it down, tear it down, right to its very foundation!”
NET.8: O daughter Babylon, soon to be devastated! How blessed will be the one who repays you for what you dished out to us!
NET.9: How blessed will be the one who grabs your babies and smashes them on a rock!
